diff --git a/outlines/prompts.py b/outlines/prompts.py
index 86519ada..f02795a4 100644
--- a/outlines/prompts.py
+++ b/outlines/prompts.py
@@ -11,6 +11,143 @@ from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Optional, Type, cast
 import jinja2
 import pydantic
 
+_FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {}
+
+
+def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None:
+    """Register a filter to make it available to all prompt templates.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    name
+        The name under which the filter will be available in templates.
+    func
+        The callable implementing the filter.
+
+    """
+    _FILTERS[name] = func
+
+
+def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None:
+    """Remove a filter from the global filter registry.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    name
+        The name of the filter to remove.
+
+    """
+    _FILTERS.pop(name, None)
+
+
+def create_jinja_env(
+    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader] = None,
+    filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None,
+) -> jinja2.Environment:
+    """Create the Jinja2 environment used to render prompt templates.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    loader
+        The Jinja2 loader to use, if any.
+    filters
+        Filters local to a single template. These take precedence over
+        globally registered filters (see `register_filter`), which in turn
+        take precedence over the built-in filters.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    The configured Jinja2 environment.
+
+    """
+    env = jinja2.Environment(
+        loader=loader,
+        trim_blocks=True,
+        lstrip_blocks=True,
+        keep_trailing_newline=True,
+        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
+    )
+    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
+    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
+    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
+    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
+    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
+    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args
+    env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
+    if filters:
+        env.filters.update(filters)
+
+    return env
+
+
+_FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {}
+
+
+def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None:
+    """Register a filter to make it available to all prompt templates.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    name
+        The name under which the filter will be available in templates.
+    func
+        The callable implementing the filter.
+
+    """
+    _FILTERS[name] = func
+
+
+def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None:
+    """Remove a filter from the global filter registry.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    name
+        The name of the filter to remove.
+
+    """
+    _FILTERS.pop(name, None)
+
+
+def create_jinja_env(
+    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader] = None,
+    filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None,
+) -> jinja2.Environment:
+    """Create the Jinja2 environment used to render prompt templates.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    loader
+        The Jinja2 loader to use, if any.
+    filters
+        Filters local to a single template. These take precedence over
+        globally registered filters (see `register_filter`), which in turn
+        take precedence over the built-in filters.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    The configured Jinja2 environment.
+
+    """
+    env = jinja2.Environment(
+        loader=loader,
+        trim_blocks=True,
+        lstrip_blocks=True,
+        keep_trailing_newline=True,
+        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
+    )
+    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
+    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
+    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
+    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
+    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
+    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args
+    env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
+    if filters:
+        env.filters.update(filters)
+
+    return env
+
 
 @dataclass
 class Prompt:
@@ -40,7 +177,7 @@ class Prompt:
             return self.template.render(**kwargs)
 
     @classmethod
-    def from_str(cls, content: str):
+    def from_str(cls, content: str, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None):
         """
         Create an instance of the class from a string.
 
@@ -48,15 +185,18 @@ class Prompt:
         ----------
         content : str
             The string content to be converted into a template.
+        filters : Optional[Dict[str, Callable]]
+            A dictionary mapping custom filter names to callables to register
+            on the Jinja2 environment used to render this template.
 
         Returns
         -------
         An instance of the class with the provided content as a template.
         """
-        return cls(cls._template_from_str(content), None)
+        return cls(cls._template_from_str(content, filters), None)
 
     @classmethod
-    def from_file(cls, path: Path):
+    def from_file(cls, path: Path, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None):
         """
         Create a Prompt instance from a file containing a Jinja template.
 
@@ -67,6 +207,9 @@
